title: podman run --security-opt label=type:svirt_qemu_net_t : AVC
body:
```console
# podman run --rm --security-opt label=type:svirt_qemu_net_t alpine date
standard_init_linux.go:211: exec user process caused "permission denied"
# echo $?
1
```
AVC is:
```
type=AVC msg=audit(1576158540.639:47074): avc:  denied  { transition } for  pid=16008 comm="runc:[2:INIT]" path="/bin/busybox" dev="overlay" ino=1626763 scontext=unconfined_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 tcontext=system_u:system_r:svirt_qemu_net_t:s0:c259,c674 tclass=process permissive=0
```
Found when testing 1.7.0 but also seems to be present in 1.6.2 and possibly older.

comments.body: This is what is supposed to happen.  svirt_qemu_net_t is a VM selinux type not a container type.  We want to block this transition.
Thanks. It works with docker (I don't know why). I'll remove that from the podman-docker-autotest suite.

I would be surprised that this works from Docker, unless docker daemon is not running as container_runtime_t.

ps -eZ | grep dockerd
I don't have a RHEL7 system to test with right now... but fwiw this is a test that you wrote for inclusion in docker-autotest in early 2016... and it has been working 100% with docker since then.

docker-1.13.1-104.git4ef4b30.el7 and container-selinux-2.107-3.el7:
```console
# docker run --rm --security-opt label=type:svirt_qemu_net_t alpine cat /proc/self/attr/current;echo
system_u:system_r:svirt_qemu_net_t:s0:c575,c667
# ps -eZ|grep dockerd
system_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 16617 ? 00:00:00 dockerd-current
```
@rhatdan I'll leave the virt running and check in again tomorrow morning in case there's anything else you'd like me to look at.

But the rhel7 system does not have the same container-selinux as we have in RHEL8.  Basically I updated the selinux-policy to NOT allow the transition from a container_runtime_t do svirt_* (Virtual machine) domains.  So this is expected.

2016 Dan Walsh was mistaken.  And hopefully 2019 Dan Walsh is a little better informed.  :^)

Makes sense. I happened to have an old RHEL8 VM handy and found that the transition was allowed with `container-selinux-2:2.107-2.module+el8.1.0+4016+4220c060` but forbidden after dnf-upgrade to `2:2.124.0-1.module+el8.2.0+5182+3136e5d4`. Thank you, 2019 @rhatdan!
